title: Ipomoea arenaria
intro: Ipomoea arenaria, also known as Beach Morning Glory, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea arenaria include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to relieve respiratory symptoms and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Ipomoea arenaria is classified within the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory family. This family comprises over 1,000 species of flowering plants. As a member of the Convolvulaceae family, Ipomoea arenaria shares characteristics with other species such as bindweeds and morning glories. The genus Ipomoea is a group of over 600 species of flowering plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Ipomoea arenaria is a member of the family Convolvulaceae and the genus Ipomoea.
uses: Ipomoea arenaria, a type of beach morning glory, is not commonly documented for its medicinal uses. However, some species in the Ipomoea genus are known for their potential health benefits. The leaves and stems of certain Ipomoea species have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
